channel.title: free range statistics
channel.description: I write about applications of data and analytical techniques like statistical modelling and simulation to real-world situations. I show how to access and use data, and provide examples of analytical products and the code that produced them.

item.title: United Kingdom prime ministers
item.description: UK has had a spurt of prime ministerial turnover in the past decade or so, but it's by no means unprecedented. I download data from Wikipedia and try several ways to visualise that turnover.

item.title: Seasonal adjustment
item.description: Seasonal adjustment is easy but getting hold of data can be hard. In this case I wrangle Samoa's monthly visitor arrivals data from 2017 to present and then use the seas and fable R packages to seasonally adjust it and ask if there is an impact from Iran war fuel crisis (there isn't evidence of one yet).

item.title: Durations of wars
item.description: Modern wars on average last longer than a year, but the distribution is very right-skewed. Conditional on a war lasting 74 days and with no information available other than past durations, the median total duration is 261 days, and an 80% prediction interval would be (95, 1,752) days.

item.title: Pacific island energy supply
item.description: Pacific island countries are heavily dependent on imported diesel, gas and kerosene for electricity generation and for cooking.

item.title: Pacific island demograpy, the narrative
item.description: Pacific island populations are shaped by people movements, more than is the case for most countries in the world. Ease of migration—because of history, culture or international agreements—has a critical impact on population size, growth and shape; and the fundamentals of the economy.

item.title: Pacific island remittances
item.description: Pacific island countries have some of the highest dependencies in the world on remittance payments from overseas.

item.title: Pacific island population pyramids (again)
item.description: Population pyramids for Pacific countries, one or two at a time. I use these to highlight contrasts between two coral atoll nations, one of which has free movement to a large, rich country, and one of which doesn't.

item.title: Pacific diaspora
item.description: Some Pacific countries have dramatically more of "their" people living overseas than in the origin country. And some don't.

item.title: The world's biggest 'Pacific' cities
item.description: My best hasty effort at presenting the cities around the world with the most Pacific Islanders resident.

item.title: Visualising income inequality social tables
item.description: Visualising income inequality social tables as a scatter plot rather than a dual axis bar/line plot. I use examples from eighteenth and nineteenth century France, England and Wales drawing on Branko Milanovic's Visions of Inequality 2023 publication.
